topic: OMNIX Evolutionary Skill Engine, Interactive Prompt Bootstrap, and Anti-Failure Persistent Memory
context: We researched the sixth reference library, the local 'Skill Library' project, to detail the self-evolving agent workflow, memory distillation, and skills fusion sandbox for OMNIX Workbench.
options_considered: Run external CLI agents with basic system prompts, allowing automatic execution without confirmation, and no mechanism to learn from past errors.

Implement: 1. Strict Interactive Prompt Bootstrap (never run automatically, prompt preview always requires manual confirm); 2. Enforced Agent Guidelines & Guardrails; 3. Self-evolving Skill Engine with D3.js topological view, Split Diff comparison, and a Public Skill Market; 4. Anti-Failure Persistent Memory Bank (ai-memory.json) which injects history lessons into CLIs (via CLAUDE.md/instructions) and offers post-task Experience Distillation.
chosen_option: Implement Interactive Bootstrap, Enforced Guidelines, Self-evolving Skill Engine, and Anti-Failure Memory with Distillation.
reasoning: Operator Control: Developers must have the final say on the initial instructions fed to an AI. Visualizing and editing the generated prompt before execution avoids wasted API tokens and unintended file changes.
Guidelines Enforcement: Infusing agents with developer standards (e.g. documentation integrity, lint checks before commit) guarantees high-quality, standardized outputs.

Anti-Failure Memory: Humans learn from mistakes; agents must too. Saving project-specific traps (e.g. 'Never use API version X in this repo') in `ai-memory.json` and injecting them into the agent's startup instructions prevents repeating costly coding errors.

Skill Evolution & Market: Allowing users to refine, merge (using Visual Diff), and dynamically combine skills for specialized tasks—coupled with a public directory for sharing—creates a powerful compound value system for developers.
tradeoffs: Pre-seeding memory files (like CLAUDE.md) might clash with manual user edits: resolved by using structured comment blocks (`# OMNIX MEMORY - DO NOT EDIT`) inside the file to isolate and update memory dynamically.
impact_scope: Tauri CLI bridge (pre-flight instructions injector, CLAUDE.md/system instructions writer)
Memory manager (experience distillation parser, ai-memory.json database)
Frontend workspace (topological graph, Split Diff view, skill market panel)
future_risks: Over-distillation generating redundant or contradictory memory entries: mitigated by providing a manual management board to delete, edit, or merge memory items.
